content: We can frame Self-Refine’s iterative self-feedback workflow as a foundational backbone for instantiating multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where each iteration of Self-Refine can be assigned a distinct inconsistent solving perspective drawn from Self-Contrast or Mirror frameworks. This creates a layered self-reflection system that retains Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervision, while addressing the limitation of single-perspective self-feedback that often misses blind spots in initial outputs.
